goal: Create the reusable Terraform ECR module with repository resources, lifecycle policies, scan-on-push, tag mutability, and outputs.
summary: Phase 1 complete: ECR module files (versions.tf, variables.tf, main.tf, outputs.tf) created and validated. terraform fmt -check and terraform validate passed inside terraform/modules/ecr/.
stepSummaries:
1. Create terraform/modules/ecr/versions.tf with Terraform >= 1.6.0 and AWS provider ~> 5.0 constraints. [verified]
  2. Create terraform/modules/ecr/variables.tf declaring project, environment, service_names, image_tag_mutability, scan_on_push, and tags variables. [verified]
  3. Create terraform/modules/ecr/main.tf using aws_ecr_repository (for_each over service_names) with image_scanning_configuration and image_tag_mutability, plus aws_ecr_lifecycle_policy resources. [verified]
  4. Create terraform/modules/ecr/outputs.tf exporting repository_urls and repository_arns maps. [verified]
  5. Validate module formatting and syntax. [verified]

reviewerRationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

gateVerdicts.rationale: All 5 steps used real verification: grep checks and Terraform fmt/init/validate.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Step 5 ran terraform fmt -check, terraform init -backend=false, and terraform validate successfully.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Combined output is a reusable ECR module with repository, lifecycle policy, scan-on-push, tag mutability, and outputs.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Files exist in terraform/modules/ecr/ and module validate succeeded.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Nothing deferred in Phase 1; wiring into environments is Phase 2.
